basics.name: Nathan A. Ritchie
basics.label: Engineering and software
basics.summary: Mechanical and materials engineering and computer science background with experience across data pipelines, dashboards, manufacturing process engineering, QA automation, embedded systems, and software projects.

work.name: Metro Vancouver Regional District
highlights: Developed Power BI dashboards to better communicate energy usage data, saving 10-15 hours of work weekly.
Built Python data pipelines to automate data refreshes for Power BI dashboards.
Applied industry-standard measurement and verification techniques to quantify performance of capital projects.

work.name: Rocket Lab Space Systems
highlights: Utilized Python and pandas to investigate product failure modes and refine safe operational envelope figures.
Automated the development of a product database with Python using PDFQuery to gather historical data that was not centrally recorded.
Developed preventative maintenance planning and automated work scheduling using Infor LN.

work.name: General Motors
highlights: Oversaw retooling of an entirely new electric vehicle body shop, including budget and roadblock management.
Implemented PLC control algorithms to introduce error proofing to production, reducing downtime and improving quality.
Managed the deployment of new network architecture to improve plant monitoring and control.
Designed fixtures for factory retooling while balancing the needs of production, management, and engineering stakeholders.
Developed LEAN manufacturing solutions for damaged parts and downtime scenarios.
Managed a group of 24 tradespeople by prioritizing work, coordinating contractors, and summarizing work for the next shift.

work.name: Rocket Lab Space Systems
highlights: Developed a laser vibrometry QA test, including concept generation, mechanical design, and data processing using Python and pandas.
Developed product functional testing scripts for thermal vacuum testing.
Designed and implemented complex qualification testing fixtures involving large assemblies and FEA analysis.
Frequently participated in soldering and wiring harness building for flight hardware and ground support equipment.
Operated power supplies, oscilloscopes, multimeters, and other diagnostic equipment.
Helped improve a new product design to improve manufacturing repeatability and minimize drift from specifications during transport.

education.area: Mechanical and Materials Engineering, Dean's Honour List

projects.name: Smart Home Controller
projects.summary: C/C++ and Qt smart home controller with a responsive GUI for real-time device information and states.
highlights: Developed in C/C++ using the Qt framework.
Designed Qt widgets for real-time smart home information and device state display.

projects.name: Heart Disease Prediction Using Machine Learning
projects.summary: Python machine learning project for heart disease prediction.
highlights: Built and optimized Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost models, achieving an AUC-ROC score of 0.948.
Conducted robust scaling, one-hot encoding, Matplotlib visualization, correlation heatmaps, and feature importance analysis.

projects.name: BeStep
projects.summary: Cross-platform social media alternative to BeReal using Flutter, Dart, Firebase, and native pedometer libraries.
highlights: Built milestone-based photo sharing triggered by step achievements.
Implemented movement tracking and Firebase authentication, image storage, and crash logging.

projects.name: Desktop Spell Checker
projects.summary: Java desktop spell checker built by a four-person Agile team.
highlights: Led Scrum process, managed product backlog, and distributed sprint tasks.
Improved architecture using OOP principles, design patterns, and Git collaboration.

projects.name: Multiplayer Online Snake Game
projects.summary: Python server-client snake game with networking and RSA-encrypted communication.
highlights: Managed multiple client connections and server-side game logic.
Added real-time public messaging and encrypted data exchange.

projects.name: Scavenger Robot
projects.summary: WiFi-operated autonomous robot using C++, ESP microcontrollers, CAN/I2C components, and PID control.
highlights: Built remote operation and autonomous object classification behavior.
Developed robust control structures for reliable autonomous operation.

projects.name: University T.A. Management Website
projects.summary: PHP, MySQL, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript web application for managing a Teaching Assistant database.
highlights: Built sorting, insertion, deletion, profile views, conditional image rendering, course preferences, TA-course assignments, and course offering views.
Focused on modularity, maintainability, and documentation.
